title: Dasystephana algida
taxonomy: Dasystephana algida is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Andes mountain range in South America. Further classification of Dasystephana algida includes its placement within the genus Dasystephana, which is part of the tribe Senecioneae. The exact classification of this species may be subject to change as new research emerges. Within the broader classification system, Dasystephana algida is a member of the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Dasystephana algida, commonly known as the "Ice Verbena," is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Verbena family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation, making it a popular remedy for gastrointestinal issues and fever relief. The bioactive constituents of Dasystephana algida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
